project: I2C Demo — Phase 3 React Flow Frontend
description: Build a React Flow-based frontend where users drag-and-drop I2C test nodes onto a canvas, connect them to define execution order, serialize the graph to JSON, and run simulations via the Phase 2 FastAPI backend.

userStories.title: Project Setup with Bun + Vite + React + TypeScript
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a properly configured frontend project so that I can start building components.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/ directory created with Bun + Vite using React + TypeScript template
Tailwind CSS v4 installed and configured (utility classes work in components)
React Flow (@xyflow/react) installed as dependency
bun dev starts the dev server without errors
bun run build produces a production build without errors
TypeScript strict mode enabled in tsconfig.json
Typecheck passes

sharedFiles.reason: Created here, later stories may add deps
Created here, US-002 will restructure it

userStories.notes: Use `bun create vite frontend --template react-ts`. Install deps: `bun add @xyflow/react`, `bun add -D tailwindcss @tailwindcss/vite`. For Tailwind v4, add the Vite plugin to vite.config.ts and import tailwindcss in the main CSS file.

userStories.title: Application Shell & Layout
userStories.description: As a user, I want a structured layout with distinct panels so that I can see all parts of the interface at once.
acceptanceCriteria: Three-panel layout: left sidebar (node palette ~200px), center (React Flow canvas, flex-grow), bottom (result panel)
Sidebar displays the 5 node types (Reset, Write, Read, Scan, Delay) as draggable items with labels and color accents
Result panel is collapsible and starts collapsed (show/hide toggle)
A top toolbar area with a Run button (disabled state initially)
Layout fills the full viewport height and width
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser: all panels render correctly at 1280x800 minimum
userStories.notes: Create components: Sidebar.tsx, Toolbar.tsx, ResultPanel.tsx. Use flexbox layout: row for sidebar+canvas, column wrapper for toolbar on top and result panel on bottom. Sidebar items should set HTML5 drag data (dataTransfer) with the node type string. React Flow canvas is a placeholder ReactFlow component with empty nodes/edges for now.

userStories.title: Custom Node Components
userStories.description: As a user, I want distinct visual nodes for each I2C operation so that I can identify them on the canvas.
acceptanceCriteria: ResetNode — displays 'Reset' label, no editable parameters, one output handle (bottom)
WriteNode — displays inline fields for address (hex input), register (hex input), data (comma-separated hex bytes input); one input handle (top), one output handle (bottom)
ReadNode — displays inline fields for address (hex), register (hex), byte count n (number), optional expected data (comma-separated hex); one input handle (top), one output handle (bottom)
ScanNode — displays inline field for address (hex), optional expected result (true/false select); one input handle (top), one output handle (bottom)
DelayNode — displays inline field for cycle count (integer input); one input handle (top), one output handle (bottom)
Each node type has a distinct border/header color for quick visual identification
Node parameters are editable directly on the node via controlled inputs that update node.data
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser: all 5 node types render with correct fields
userStories.notes: Create files in frontend/src/components/nodes/: ResetNode.tsx, WriteNode.tsx, ReadNode.tsx, ScanNode.tsx, DelayNode.tsx. Register all node types in a nodeTypes map passed to ReactFlow. Use React Flow's Handle component for input/output handles (Position.Top for input, Position.Bottom for output). Store parameters in node.data and use useReactFlow().setNodes to update on input change. Suggested colors: Reset=slate, Write=blue, Read=green, Scan=amber, Delay=purple.

userStories.title: Drag-and-Drop from Sidebar to Canvas
userStories.description: As a user, I want to drag node types from the sidebar onto the canvas so that I can build test sequences visually.
acceptanceCriteria: Dragging a node type from the sidebar and dropping it on the canvas creates a new node instance of that type
The new node appears at the drop position with default parameter values
Default values: address=0x50, register=0x00, data=[], n=1, cycles=100
Multiple instances of the same node type can be added
Nodes can be repositioned on the canvas after placement
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser: drag-and-drop works smoothly

sharedFiles.reason: Add onDragOver/onDrop handlers to ReactFlow wrapper

userStories.notes: Implement onDragOver (preventDefault + set dropEffect) and onDrop handlers on the ReactFlow wrapper component. In onDrop: get node type from dataTransfer, use reactFlowInstance.screenToFlowPosition() to convert drop coordinates, create new node with unique ID (e.g., `${type}-${Date.now()}`), add to nodes state. Sidebar items should use onDragStart to set dataTransfer.setData('application/reactflow', nodeType).

userStories.title: Node Connection & Edge Management
userStories.description: As a user, I want to connect nodes with edges to define execution order so that I can create sequential test flows.
acceptanceCriteria: Dragging from an output handle to an input handle creates a directed edge
Edges render as smoothstep type with animated markerEnd arrows
Each output handle allows only one outgoing edge (enforced via onConnect validation)
Each input handle allows only one incoming edge (enforced via onConnect validation)
Edges can be deleted by selecting and pressing Delete/Backspace
Nodes can be deleted by selecting and pressing Delete/Backspace
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser: connections form correctly and deletions work
userStories.notes: Use React Flow's onConnect callback. Before adding an edge, check that the source node doesn't already have an outgoing edge and the target node doesn't already have an incoming edge — if so, replace the existing edge. Set defaultEdgeOptions with type='smoothstep' and markerEnd={type: MarkerType.ArrowClosed}. Use onNodesDelete and onEdgesDelete for delete key handling (React Flow handles this automatically when deleteKeyCode is set).

userStories.title: Flow-to-JSON Serialization
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to convert the React Flow graph into the backend's JSON step format so that I can submit it for simulation.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/src/lib/serialize.ts exports a function serializeFlow(nodes, edges) that returns an array of step objects
Performs topological sort on the directed graph to determine execution order
Disconnected nodes are excluded — only the longest connected chain starting from a root node (no incoming edges) is serialized

Each node maps to its corresponding step format: reset={op:'reset'}, write={op:'write_bytes',addr,reg,data}, read={op:'read_bytes',addr,reg,n,expect?}, scan={op:'scan',addr,expect?}, delay={op:'delay',cycles}
Hex string values are formatted as '0x' prefix (e.g., '0x50', '0xA5')
Unit tests pass for: linear chain, single node, empty canvas, disconnected nodes filtering
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Topological sort: build adjacency list from edges, find root nodes (no incoming edges), BFS/DFS from the root. If multiple roots exist, pick the longest chain. Map node.type + node.data to the backend step format. For write/read/scan nodes, format addr and reg as hex strings. For write data and read expect, split comma-separated input and format each as hex string. Tests: use vitest (add as dev dependency), test file at frontend/src/lib/serialize.test.ts.

userStories.title: API Client & Run Execution
userStories.description: As a user, I want to click Run to execute my test flow and see results so that I can validate my I2C sequences.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/src/lib/api.ts exports functions: runSimulation(steps), fetchTemplates(), getWaveformUrl(id)
API base URL is configurable via VITE_API_URL env var (defaults to http://localhost:8000/api)
Run button in toolbar is enabled only when canvas has at least one connected chain (2+ nodes connected by edge)
Clicking Run serializes the flow, calls POST /api/run, and shows a loading spinner on the button
On success, simulation result is stored in app state for ResultPanel to display
On error (422/500/503), an inline error banner is shown below the toolbar with the error message
Error banner can be dismissed
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser: Run button triggers API call and shows loading state

sharedFiles.reason: Wire up Run button click handler with serialize + API call + state management

userStories.notes: Use fetch() for API calls (no need for axios). API client pattern: const API_BASE = import.meta.env.VITE_API_URL || 'http://localhost:8000/api'. Store simulation result in React state (useState<SimulationResult | null>). Pass result down to ResultPanel. For Vite dev proxy alternative: add proxy config in vite.config.ts to forward /api to localhost:8000.

userStories.title: Result Panel — Step Results & Register Dump
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see detailed execution results so that I can verify my I2C test passed or failed.
acceptanceCriteria: Result panel auto-expands when simulation results are available
Shows overall pass/fail status with green (pass) or red (fail) color indicator and text
Each step is listed with: step index, operation name, status (ok/fail), and relevant details
For read_bytes steps: shows returned data array and match result (pass/fail vs expected)
For scan steps: shows found status and match result
Failed steps are highlighted with red background
Register dump section shows a formatted hex table (address: value) of the 256-byte register file
A 'Download VCD' button triggers waveform file download via GET /api/waveform/{waveform_id}
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser: results display correctly for both pass and fail scenarios
userStories.notes: ResultPanel receives simulation result as prop. Register dump: display as a grid of 16 columns x 16 rows (256 bytes). Download VCD: use window.open() or create an <a> tag with the waveform URL from api.ts getWaveformUrl(). Show 'No results yet' message when result is null.

userStories.title: Node Status Overlay
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see pass/fail status on each node after execution so that I can quickly identify which step failed.
acceptanceCriteria: After simulation completes, each node in the executed chain shows a small status badge (green checkmark for ok, red X for fail)
Status is mapped by step index to the corresponding node in the serialized topological order
Status badges clear when the flow is modified (node added/removed/parameter edited, edge added/removed)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser: status badges appear on nodes after running a simulation and clear on edit

sharedFiles.reason: Add status state management and clear-on-edit logic

userStories.notes: Store step results in a Map<nodeId, 'ok' | 'fail'> state. After simulation, map the serialized node order to step results. Pass status to each node via node.data.status. In custom node components, render a small badge based on data.status. Clear the status map in onNodesChange, onEdgesChange, and when node data changes.

userStories.title: Template Loading
userStories.description: As a user, I want to load pre-built test templates so that I can quickly start with common test patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Templates' dropdown button in the toolbar lists templates fetched from GET /api/templates
Template list shows template name and description
Selecting a template fetches full template via GET /api/templates/{id} and populates the canvas with corresponding nodes and edges
Template steps are auto-positioned in a vertical chain (spaced evenly, ~120px apart vertically)
Loading a template shows a confirmation dialog if the canvas is not empty ('Replace current flow?')
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser: templates load and display correctly on canvas
userStories.notes: Fetch templates on component mount or on dropdown open. To convert template steps to nodes: iterate steps, create a node for each step with appropriate type and data, position at (250, i * 120), create edges connecting sequential nodes. Use the same node type mapping as serialize.ts but in reverse (op -> node type). Template dropdown can be a simple div with absolute positioning, no need for a modal library.

userStories.title: localStorage Flow Persistence
userStories.description: As a user, I want my flow to persist across page refreshes so that I don't lose my work.
acceptanceCriteria: Current flow (nodes array, edges array, viewport transform) is auto-saved to localStorage on every change
On page load, saved flow is restored from localStorage if present
A 'Clear' button in the toolbar resets the canvas to empty and clears localStorage
Saved data is stored under the key 'i2c-demo-flow'
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser: refresh preserves the flow; Clear button removes it

sharedFiles.reason: Add localStorage save/restore logic and Clear button

userStories.notes: Use useEffect to save to localStorage when nodes/edges change (debounce with setTimeout ~500ms to avoid excessive writes). On mount, read from localStorage and set initial nodes/edges/viewport. Use ReactFlow's onMoveEnd or fitView for viewport restore. Store as JSON: {nodes, edges, viewport}. Clear button: setNodes([]), setEdges([]), localStorage.removeItem('i2c-demo-flow').

userStories.title: Input Validation & UX Polish
userStories.description: As a user, I want clear feedback when I enter invalid parameters so that I can fix issues before running.
acceptanceCriteria: Address fields validate hex format in range 0x00-0x7F (7-bit I2C address)
Register fields validate hex format in range 0x00-0xFF
Data fields validate as comma-separated hex bytes (each 0x00-0xFF)
Cycle count validates as positive integer (> 0)
Invalid fields show a red border and brief inline error text below the input
Run button is disabled if any node in the connected chain has validation errors
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser: validation errors display correctly for invalid inputs
userStories.notes: Create a shared validation utility (frontend/src/lib/validate.ts) with functions: validateHexAddr(v), validateHexReg(v), validateHexDataList(v), validatePositiveInt(v). Each returns {valid: boolean, error?: string}. Call validators in node components on input change, store validation state in node.data.errors. In the Run handler, check all nodes for errors before serializing. Add 'nodrag' class to input elements inside nodes to prevent drag when interacting with form fields.
